completed_tasks.name: TDD RED tests + isMultiPointMarkup type guard + MarkupCommand reopen-recommit variant
useMarkupTool activatePreset extension + Ctrl+Z dispatch + CanvasViewport reopen handler + App.tsx toast

remaining_tasks.name: Spawn gsd-code-fixer for CR-01 + CR-02 + WR-01 + WR-02 + WR-03 (user-approved scope)
Re-run vitest after fixes; confirm 23/23 still pass + no regressions
Spawn gsd-verifier — goal-backward verification against ROADMAP.md phase 13 goal
Start dev server and verify Ctrl+Z re-open works end-to-end in CanvasViewport (the bugs CR-01/CR-02 only manifest at integration level)
Run phase.complete via gsd-tools; update STATE/ROADMAP/REQUIREMENTS

blockers.description: CR-01 (Blocker): CanvasViewport reopen handler does not call setActiveTool(tool). After Ctrl+Z the tool-sync useEffect at CanvasViewport.tsx:516-522 fires cancelMarkup() and wipes the seeded state. Unit tests miss this (HookHost-only).
blockers.workaround: Spawn gsd-code-fixer; add setActiveTool(tool) before/inside the activatePreset call in the reopen handler. Mirror App.tsx:281-289 setChainArmedFromTotals coupling pattern.

blockers.description: CR-02 (Blocker): useMarkupTool.ts:398-401 popLastPoint calls cancel() on last-vertex pop but cancel() does not clear _reopenSnapshot. During re-open, popping all vertices orphans the snapshot — a subsequent Ctrl+Z overwrites it and the original markup is lost forever (removeForReopen already ran).
blockers.workaround: Spawn gsd-code-fixer; either (a) make cancel() clear _reopenSnapshot too, or (b) add explicit setReopenSnapshot(null) in the popLastPoint last-vertex branch.

human_actions_pending.action: Browser UAT — load a PDF, draw a multi-point markup (linear/area/perimeter/wall), commit with Enter, press Ctrl+Z, confirm shape re-opens in drawing mode with points seeded + toast appears. Try Esc to restore, Ctrl+Z then page-nav (implicit Esc), and full pop-all-points edge case.
human_actions_pending.context: CLAUDE.md mandates browser testing for UI changes. CR-01 in particular would not appear in unit tests (HookHost-only) — only by exercising CanvasViewport mounted via the real App tree.

decisions.decision: User chose 'Fix blockers + real warnings, then verify' from the 3-option presentation
decisions.rationale: 2 blockers (CR-01 self-cancel, CR-02 snapshot orphan) are real browser-broken bugs. WR-01/02/03 are real bugs too (cap bypass, store-reset leak, missing deps). WR-04/05 + IN-01/02/03 deferred as cosmetic/style.

decisions.decision: Mode=yolo + no branching — all 3 plans merged directly to master
decisions.rationale: Project config: branching_strategy=none, mode=yolo. Confirmed in .planning/config.json.

decisions.decision: Worktree isolation used despite single-plan-per-wave layout
decisions.rationale: workflow.use_worktrees defaults to true. Worked correctly for Waves 1 and 2; Wave 0 had a pre-reset edit leak (anti-pattern recorded below) that was caught and resolved manually.

next_action: 1) Stage and WIP-commit 13-REVIEW.md. 2) Spawn gsd-code-fixer with scope = CR-01, CR-02, WR-01, WR-02, WR-03 from 13-REVIEW.md. 3) Re-run vitest + tsc. 4) Spawn gsd-verifier with phase_dir=.planning/phases/13-post-commit-step-level-undo. 5) On pass, run phase.complete via gsd-tools.
context_notes: Phase 13 implements post-commit step-level undo (Ctrl+Z on a committed multi-point markup re-opens it in drawing mode). All 3 plans landed + merged. Unit suite 534/534 GREEN. But code-reviewer caught 2 integration-level blockers that unit tests cannot reach (CanvasViewport is never mounted in the test file). User has approved fixing these. Verifier has not yet run — fixes must land before verification + UAT.
